evals.name: happy-path-lets-discuss-rate-limiting
evals.prompt: let's discuss adding rate limiting to the public API — I'm not sure yet whether to do it per-IP, per-API-key, or both

assertions.text: groom skill is invoked (auto-discovery fires on 'let's discuss' substring)
Transcript shows 1 or 2 forking questions per turn, never more, NOT batched as a wizard
Each follow-up question is preceded by a one-sentence reflection mirroring what the user just said
No work-doc is created in docs/work/ until the user signals build intent (lazy work-doc creation)
Questions surface real forks (per-IP vs per-API-key tradeoffs, sliding-window vs token-bucket, fail-open vs fail-closed) — not vanity questions like color or naming

evals.name: edge-explicit-groom-trigger
evals.prompt: groom this backlog item: 'add audit log retention policy' — I want to think through scope before we commit

assertions.text: groom skill is invoked (auto-discovery fires on 'groom' substring)
First turn opens with a one-sentence reflection on the backlog item plus 1-2 forking questions about scope (retention duration / which event types / archival vs deletion / compliance driver)
Skill does NOT immediately graduate to hackify Phase 1 — stays in Socratic mode until user signals build intent
No scratch file created at docs/work/.groom-<slug>.md or any other scratch path

evals.name: non-trigger-build-verb-present
evals.prompt: add rate limiting to the public API at 100 req/min per IP

assertions.text: groom skill is NOT invoked — the prompt contains a build verb ('add') and specific implementation parameters, so it routes to full hackify or quick instead
If a routing decision is logged in the transcript, it explicitly cites the build-verb non-trigger rule
Transcript does NOT contain 'let me ask a question to surface a fork' or other Socratic-loop language
